title: Generating Thoughts as Candidates
concept: Prompting the model to generate 3-5 candidate steps for the next phase of a problem.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, how does Tree of Thoughts represent an AI's reasoning?

options.text: As branches in a tree, so it can explore several paths at once
As one single straight line with no choices
As a random pile of unrelated facts

exercises.explain: Tree of Thoughts treats reasoning steps as branches in a tree. That lets the agent explore multiple possible paths instead of being stuck on just one.

cards.title: One Step, Many Guesses
cards.body: Before an AI picks its next move, it can first brainstorm a handful of options. Think of it as sprouting several tiny branches before choosing which one to grow.

cards.title: What Is Generating Candidates?
cards.body: It means asking the model to produce 3 to 5 possible next steps for a problem, instead of just one. Each option is called a candidate thought. Later, the agent will judge which ones are worth exploring.

cards.title: Like Sampling at an Ice Cream Shop
cards.body: You don't buy the first flavor you see. You ask for a few small tasters first, then decide. Generating candidates lets the AI 'taste' several next steps before committing.

cards.title: A Math Puzzle
cards.body: Given the numbers 4, 9, and 10 to make 24, the AI generates candidates: 'try 9 + 10', 'try 10 - 4', 'try 4 x 9'. Three next steps, each a branch to explore.

cards.title: More Isn't Always Better
cards.body: You might think generating 50 candidates makes the AI smarter. But too many options waste time and cost. Usually 3 to 5 solid candidates is the sweet spot.

cards.title: One Path vs Many Candidates
cards.body: A basic prompt commits to one guess. Tree of Thoughts asks for several candidates first, keeping its options open.

left.text: Model writes just one next step and hopes it's right.

right.text: Model lists 3-5 next steps to compare before choosing.

exercises.prompt: When 'generating thoughts as candidates', what is the AI actually being asked to do?

options.text: Produce 3-5 possible next steps for the problem
Repeat the exact same step over and over

exercises.explain: Generating candidates means the model brainstorms a small set (usually 3-5) of possible next steps, giving the agent several branches to explore later.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Generating hundreds of candidate thoughts is always better than generating just a few.
exercises.explain: False. Too many candidates waste time and money. A small set of 3-5 strong options is usually the sweet spot.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about candidate generation.
exercises.sentence: Instead of one guess, the model produces several candidate steps to explore.
exercises.explain: Each option the model proposes is a candidate step, one possible branch the agent might explore next.

exercises.prompt: Which of these looks like an AI-generated list of candidate next steps for solving a maze?

options.text: Option 1: turn left at the fork. Option 2: go straight ahead. Option 3: backtrack to the last junction.
I got lost in a maze last summer and it was really scary.
Mazes are a type of puzzle found in many old gardens.

exercises.explain: The AI candidate list offers several clear, comparable next moves (turn left, go straight, backtrack) which is exactly what generating candidate thoughts produces.

exercises.prompt: An AI is solving a riddle using Tree of Thoughts. It's at the point where it needs the NEXT step. What should the 'generate candidates' stage do, and why?

options.text: Produce 3-5 different possible next steps so the agent has several branches to compare and explore
Immediately commit to the single first idea it thinks of
Generate 500 next steps to be as thorough as possible
Skip the next step and jump straight to the final answer

exercises.explain: The candidate stage prompts the model to brainstorm a small set of 3-5 possible next steps. This gives the tree several branches to weigh, without overwhelming it with too many options or gambling everything on a single guess. Keeping the set small but varied is the balance that makes Tree of Thoughts powerful.
